Barnet.battery: Do not put batteries inside any household bin. Use supermarket battery takeback or Barnet’s Summers Lane Reuse and Recycling Centre; some properties can set batteries out for collection—see barnet.gov.uk recycling pages.
Barnet.biological: Many Barnet homes do not have a separate food waste collection—check barnet.gov.uk for your property. Food scraps and soiled packaging usually belong in the black refuse bin unless you have a food or garden service.
Barnet.cardboard: Clean, dry cardboard (flattened) goes in the blue mixed recycling bin; very large sheets can be placed beside the bin. Keep cardboard free of food and grease.
Barnet.glass: Rinse glass bottles and jars and place them in the blue mixed recycling bin (lids can be left on or off per Barnet guidance).
Barnet.metal: Rinse food tins, drink cans, empty aerosols, and foil trays and place them loose in the blue mixed recycling bin.
Barnet.paper: Clean mixed paper and envelopes go in the blue bin. Shredded paper must be sealed inside a paper envelope before recycling.
Barnet.plastic: Rinse plastic bottles, pots, tubs, and trays and put them in the blue bin; remove plastic film from trays and put film in refuse. No plastic bags or polystyrene in recycling.
Barnet.clothes: Textiles do not go in recycling bins—donate good items or use textile banks; large volumes can go to Summers Lane.
Barnet.shoes: Reusable shoes should be donated or taken to textile/shoe banks—not placed in the blue recycling bin.
Barnet.trash: Non-recyclables, contaminated items, nappies, and plastic film go in the black refuse bin. Check the North London Waste Authority A–Z for unusual items.

Camden.battery: Never put batteries in rubbish or recycling bins (fire risk). Use supermarket drop-offs, order a Veolia battery bag, or take them to Regis Road Reuse and Recycling Centre—see camden.gov.uk.
Camden.biological: Use your food waste caddies where provided: raw/cooked food, peelings, bones, dairy, and teabags go in the food waste service (compostable liner, paper, or no liner—not black bags)—see camden.gov.uk/what-goes-in-bins.
Camden.cardboard: Clean cardboard and cartons go in your recycling bin or bags with other dry recyclables; flatten boxes and keep them dry.
Camden.glass: Rinse glass bottles and jars and place them in your recycling collection.
Camden.metal: Rinse tins, cans, aerosols, aluminium foil, and bottle tops and put them in your recycling bin or bags.
Camden.paper: Newspapers, magazines, letters, and clean paper go in recycling; keep paper dry and not food-soiled.
Camden.plastic: Empty and rinse plastic bottles, pots, tubs, and cartons; Camden also accepts empty plastic carrier bags in recycling—see what goes in my bin.
Camden.clothes: Order textile recycling bags from Veolia or use charity banks—textiles must not go in mixed recycling.
Camden.shoes: Donate wearable shoes or use textile collections; do not place them in recycling bins.
Camden.trash: Cling film, polystyrene, nappies, crisp packets, and other non-recyclables go in your rubbish bin or bags.

Greenwich.battery: Batteries must not go in any kerbside bin. Take them to the Reuse and Recycling Centre or suitable retail collection points.
Greenwich.biological: Food and garden waste go in the green-top bin or approved paper/corn starch sacks—loose or lined with newspaper/compostable bags; no plastic bin liners.
Greenwich.cardboard: Clean, dry cardboard goes loose in the blue-top recycling bin (rinse containers; do not bag recycling).
Greenwich.glass: Rinse glass bottles and jars and place them loose in the blue-top recycling bin.
Greenwich.metal: Rinse tins, cans, empty aerosols, and foil meal containers and place them loose in the blue-top bin.
Greenwich.paper: Paper, magazines, envelopes, and non-glitter cards go in the blue-top recycling bin if clean and dry.
Greenwich.plastic: Plastic bottles, pots, and trays (not black plastic) go in the blue-top bin; plastic film, pouches, and carrier bags belong in the black-top refuse bin.
Greenwich.clothes: Textiles are not accepted in the blue bin—use textile banks, charities, or the reuse and recycling centre.
Greenwich.shoes: Donate reusable shoes or use textile banks; not for the blue recycling bin.
Greenwich.trash: Non-recyclables go in the black-top bin (bagged). Electricals, wood, and building waste need separate arrangements—see royalgreenwich.gov.uk.

Hackney.battery: Do not put batteries in sacks or bins. Use retailer takeback or North London guidance—see hackney.gov.uk / NLWA A–Z.
Hackney.biological: Food waste goes in your caddy then outdoor/communal food bin: use compostable liners, newspaper, or no liner—never plastic bags; see hackney.gov.uk/food-waste.
Hackney.cardboard: Clean card and cartons go in green recycling bags or communal recycling if dry; greasy or food-stained card belongs in rubbish.
Hackney.glass: Rinse glass bottles and jars and place them in your recycling bag or communal recycling.
Hackney.metal: Rinse tins, cans, aerosols, and foil trays and put them in recycling (empty aerosols only).
Hackney.paper: Clean paper and card go in recycling; dirty paper (e.g. greasy) goes in rubbish.
Hackney.plastic: Rinse plastic bottles, pots, and trays for recycling; flexible plastics, films, and crisp packets go in rubbish.
Hackney.clothes: Use textile banks or reuse services—clothes are not collected in mixed recycling.
Hackney.shoes: Donate or use textile banks; do not put shoes in recycling sacks.
Hackney.trash: Nappies, wipes, films, polystyrene, and non-recyclables go in black rubbish bins or bags.

Lambeth.battery: Undamaged household batteries (not car or phone/laptop lithium-ion packs) can be left in a loosely tied carrier bag on top of your green recycling bin—never inside the bin. See lambeth.gov.uk for details.
Lambeth.biological: Use your food waste bin for meat, fish, dairy, veg, bread, rice, teabags, and plate scrapings—order free caddies if needed.
Lambeth.cardboard: Clean paper and card go loose in the green recycling bin or clear council sacks; flatten boxes and keep them dry.
Lambeth.glass: Rinse jars and bottles and place them in the green recycling bin; put metal lids in separately per Lambeth guidance.
Lambeth.metal: Food and drink cans go in the green recycling bin. Foil and some metal packaging may need alternative recycling—check Lambeth’s A–Z.
Lambeth.paper: Dry newspapers, magazines, and card go in green recycling; food-soiled paper or glitter cards belong in rubbish.
Lambeth.plastic: Rinse plastic bottles (lids on), pots, tubs, trays, and drink cartons for the green bin—no plastic film, crisp packets, or black sacks as liners.
Lambeth.clothes: Clothes and shoes are not accepted in the green bin—use textile banks or council textile services.
Lambeth.shoes: Donate wearable shoes or use textile collections; not for kerbside recycling.
Lambeth.trash: Non-recyclables, films, polystyrene, nappies, and contaminated items go in the black rubbish bin.
